description: editor_update_note_body unconditionally sets is_dirty = true at editor.rs:728 without detecting whether a false→true transition actually occurred. REQ-002 item 4 specifies: 'If this is the first update for this noteId (isDirty transitions false→true), emit an editing_session_state_changed event.' REQ-004 item 5 adds: 'When isDirty transitions (true→false or false→true), emit editing_session_state_changed ... No event emitted on redundant updates (keystroke when already dirty).' The current implementation lacks transition detection logic — it always sets is_dirty = true regardless of prior state. This means even though events aren't being emitted (FIND-001), the code has no mechanism to distinguish between the first keystroke (which should trigger an event) and subsequent keystrokes (which should not).
expected: Before setting is_dirty=true, capture the prior is_dirty value. If prior was false, this is a false→true transition that should trigger event emission. If prior was already true, the update should be a no-op for is_dirty (it stays true) and no event should be emitted.
